title: Not I Barbecue

jokes.text: ...and he's not allowed to listen to that god-awful Biggie Smalls.
jokes.setup: Carlton describing his bet with Will about music preferences
jokes.punchline: Carlton calling Biggie Smalls 'god-awful' - showing his complete disconnect from hip-hop culture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton characterization - his disdain for hip-hop culture delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Tom Jones is the King of Swing. The Tower of Power. Mr. Vegas. This guy's a legend.
jokes.setup: Nicky asks who Tom Jones is
jokes.punchline: Carlton's overly dramatic, flowery description with ridiculous nicknames
jokes.explanation: Pure Carlton - his earnest reverence for dated pop culture with grandiose descriptors

jokes.text: Well, I guess one song wouldn't hurt.
jokes.setup: Carlton explaining he can't play Tom Jones due to the bet
jokes.punchline: Immediately caving to temptation after claiming he'll win
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Carlton's weakness, economically shows his lack of willpower

jokes.text: Just couldn't hang on there could you, little fella?
jokes.setup: Carlton has clearly been caught listening to Tom Jones
jokes.punchline: Will's condescending mockery calling Carlton 'little fella'
jokes.explanation: Solid payoff to the setup, Will's smug victory tone is in character

jokes.text: Five bucks? What do I look like? Come on, man. You got to break me off some.
jokes.setup: Will gives someone five dollars
jokes.punchline: The recipient demanding more money with street slang
jokes.explanation: Decent street culture humor, 'break me off some' is period-appropriate slang

jokes.text: You know, I'm gonna have to take a shower. You wanna join me? What am I saying? It's even infected me.
jokes.setup: Carlton feeling dirty from thinking about the strip club movie
jokes.punchline: Accidentally propositioning Will, then realizing the 'corruption' has affected him
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Carlton's innocent shower suggestion becomes accidentally suggestive, his horror at his own words is perfect

jokes.text: What does that make it, eight times, Will? Eight times? You told me you hated it.
jokes.setup: Will claiming to hate the movie Showgirls
jokes.punchline: Hillary revealing he's seen it eight times
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff exposing Will's hypocrisy, economically reveals his true motivations

jokes.text: No, baby, I did. I mean... And every time, I wanted to just walk out of there.
jokes.setup: Will trying to explain why he kept seeing a movie he claims to hate
jokes.punchline: His weak, unconvincing excuse that makes him look worse
jokes.explanation: Will's uncomfortable backpedaling creates cringe humor, shows his desperation

jokes.text: You never stopped asking from the moment I started here.
jokes.setup: Hillary asking when she ever asked Geoffrey for anything
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry rebuttal revealing she constantly makes demands
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's deadpan delivery and Hillary's obliviousness to her own behavior

jokes.text: I spent my first seven hours of service here installing wall-to-wall carpet for Barbie's Dream House.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey recounting Hillary's ridiculous requests
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of a butler installing doll house carpet
jokes.explanation: Wonderful absurdist image that perfectly captures Hillary's childishness and Geoffrey's long-suffering nature

jokes.text: Well, it's not my fault that when Ken got home, he didn't like it.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey mentioning the carpet had to be removed
jokes.punchline: Hillary treating dolls like real people with preferences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Hillary's complete delusion about dolls having opinions is peak character comedy

jokes.text: Geoffrey, Skipper died in that fire.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey mentioning the dollhouse burned down
jokes.punchline: Hillary mourning a doll's 'death' with genuine emotion
jokes.explanation: Peak Hillary absurdity - treating a doll's destruction as tragic death with perfect comedic timing and character consistency

jokes.text: They're right here, big guy. I've crossed out what didn't work and rewritten the rest.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asking about his speech notes
jokes.punchline: Will revealing he edited Phil's entire speech
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Will's presumptuous editing, shows his confidence and Phil's coming reaction

jokes.text: Mom, Dad said it's okay for me to use your car tonight. I didn't say that. Those weren't your exact words, Uncle Phil, but you kind of did imply that...
jokes.setup: Ashley asking to use parents' cars
jokes.punchline: Will jumping in to help manipulate Phil's words, then realizing his mistake
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic comedy, Will's impulsive help backfires in realistic way

jokes.text: You know, there was absolutely no reason for me to jump in here, was there?
jokes.setup: Will realizing he made Ashley's situation worse
jokes.punchline: His meta-commentary acknowledging his unnecessary involvement
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware comedy, Will's realization of his mistake feels natural and funny

jokes.text: None whatsoever. I'm gonna go ahead and jump out of here. Yes, jump on with your bad self.
jokes.setup: Will using 'jump' metaphorically about leaving the conversation
jokes.punchline: Phil playing on the word 'jump' with '90s slang
jokes.explanation: Phil trying to be hip with dated slang is perfectly in character, mild wordplay on 'jump'

jokes.text: Yeah, I can finally finish my speech.
jokes.setup: Vivian hinting at romance with empty house
jokes.punchline: Phil completely missing the hint and thinking about work
jokes.explanation: Classic married couple miscommunication, Phil's obliviousness is perfectly timed and in character

jokes.text: Drank hot chocolate. Had pillow fights. Watched the O.J. trial. What a night.
jokes.setup: Will describing his first sleepover as innocent childhood fun
jokes.punchline: Including watching a murder trial as part of the fun activities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the O.J. trial as childhood entertainment is wonderfully twisted and unexpected

jokes.text: Darn, another chain letter.
jokes.setup: Carlton receiving a special delivery letter with no return address
jokes.punchline: Misinterpreting a romantic invitation as junk mail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton obliviousness to romance, his literal interpretation is endearingly clueless

jokes.text: Sweet cheeks of Buddha, you pack a wallop.
jokes.setup: Michelle hits Carlton to get his attention
jokes.punchline: Carlton's bizarre, nerdy exclamation mixing sacred and profane
jokes.explanation: Absolutely perfect Carlton line - weird, unexpected, character-specific, and incredibly quotable. Pure comedy gold.

jokes.text: For some, it's bath night. For me, it's math night.
jokes.setup: Michelle asking why Carlton studies on Saturday night
jokes.punchline: Carlton's nerdy wordplay explaining his priorities
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that perfectly captures Carlton's nerdiness and social awkwardness. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Hmm. She seems a bit of a gloomy Gus.
jokes.setup: Michelle leaving disappointed after Carlton rejects her invitation
jokes.punchline: Carlton's massive understatement using old-fashioned expression
jokes.explanation: Carlton's complete obliviousness to Michelle's disappointment, using quaint language

jokes.text: For someone who's had four children, you look amazing.
jokes.setup: Phil trying to be romantic with Vivian
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment focusing on her having given birth
jokes.explanation: Classic married man mistake - compliment that accidentally emphasizes age/wear, creates cringe

jokes.text: I was just trying to punish myself.
jokes.setup: Will explaining why he kept seeing a movie he claims to hate
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous psychological explanation for obvious behavior
jokes.explanation: Will's desperate rationalization of his behavior creates awkward humor

jokes.text: All right, baby, you're the breast... Best! Best! I said best!
jokes.setup: Will trying to end phone call smoothly
jokes.punchline: Freudian slip revealing what's really on his mind
jokes.explanation: Classic Freudian slip comedy, Will's panic at his mistake adds to the humor

jokes.text: As luck would have it, it's come to my attention that there's this cute little mail carrier that's available.
jokes.setup: Will threatening Carlton to get him to go on the double date
jokes.punchline: Threatening to set up Michelle with someone else
jokes.explanation: Will's manipulative tactics showing his scheming nature, decent threat humor

jokes.text: What if I could promise you a terrible time?
jokes.setup: Carlton not wanting to go out and have fun without Michelle
jokes.punchline: Will offering the opposite - guaranteeing a bad time
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reverse psychology humor - promising the opposite of what's normally desired is unexpected and economical

jokes.text: I guarantee you the worst time that you have ever had in your life.
jokes.setup: Will's offer to promise Carlton a terrible time
jokes.punchline: Doubling down with an even stronger guarantee
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the reverse psychology concept, Will's confidence in promising misery

jokes.text: Don't stop. Go on with whatever you're doing. Wait. Oh, my God. Ew. What were you doing? I don't even wanna think about it.
jokes.setup: Hillary walking in on her parents' romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Her disgust at imagining what they were doing
jokes.explanation: Standard adult child walking in on parents comedy, Hillary's revulsion is in character

jokes.text: I'm pretty sure he was wearing pants.
jokes.setup: Hillary asking where Geoffrey is
jokes.punchline: Phil's literal interpretation about Geoffrey's clothing
jokes.explanation: Phil's dry, literal response to Hillary's question creates unexpected humor

jokes.text: Uhh, as a child, he was dropped on his tongue.
jokes.setup: Girl asking why Carlton doesn't talk much
jokes.punchline: Will's creative variation on 'dropped on his head' joke
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on a familiar insult, explains Carlton's quietness with creative wordplay

jokes.text: I'm a tutti-frutti man myself.
jokes.setup: Girl offering Carlton chocolate ice cream in suggestive way
jokes.punchline: Carlton's innocent response about ice cream flavors
jokes.explanation: Carlton's obliviousness to sexual innuendo is perfect character comedy, innocent response to suggestive setup

jokes.text: What the heck is wrong with you, Carlton? That girl just offered you two scoops.
jokes.setup: Carlton rejecting the girl's suggestive ice cream offer
jokes.punchline: Will pointing out what Carlton missed using ice cream metaphor
jokes.explanation: Will's frustration with Carlton's obliviousness, using the ice cream metaphor sexually

jokes.text: I'm afraid I'm a one-gal guy.
jokes.setup: Will pushing Carlton to engage with the girl
jokes.punchline: Carlton's loyalty to Michelle expressed in old-fashioned terms
jokes.explanation: Carlton's earnest loyalty expressed in quaint language, perfectly in character

jokes.text: It involves the word 'strip.' Set it up.
jokes.setup: Discussion of playing a word game
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate enthusiasm when 'strip' is mentioned
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Will's quick attitude change. His eagerness is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Eenie, meenie, minie, mo... now some of y'all's clothes gots to go.
jokes.setup: Will getting good tiles in strip scrabble
jokes.punchline: Adapting childhood rhyme for adult situation
jokes.explanation: Creative adaptation of innocent childhood rhyme for suggestive adult context

jokes.text: Mad Dog? Mad Dog?
jokes.setup: Learning the boyfriend's name is Mad Dog
jokes.punchline: Both characters' terrified repetition of the intimidating name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of tension, the name itself creates fear in both characters

jokes.text: That is if your breath don't kill us first.
jokes.setup: Carlton saying Mad Dog will kill them
jokes.punchline: Adding insult about Will's breath being equally deadly
jokes.explanation: Carlton getting petty insults in during crisis, showing his frustration with Will

jokes.text: Mad Dog. I'm sure he's nothing but a pussy cat.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to calm his fears about Mad Dog
jokes.punchline: Using opposite animal metaphor while being terrified
jokes.explanation: Good animal wordplay contrasting 'Mad Dog' with 'pussy cat', Carlton's nervous optimism

jokes.text: He's gonna eat me. And then he gonna floss with me.
jokes.setup: Carlton's fear of Mad Dog
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation of being eaten then used as dental floss
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - the flossing detail is unexpected and perfectly Carlton's dramatic imagination

jokes.text: As soon as the Black Ness Monster explains, we'll be home free.
jokes.setup: Mad Dog explaining something to his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Will's reference to Loch Ness Monster with racial twist
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay on famous monster, though relies on physical description for humor

jokes.text: Go on without me. Save yourself.
jokes.setup: Carlton presumably gets stuck or trapped
jokes.punchline: Dramatic movie heroic sacrifice dialogue in mundane situation
jokes.explanation: Good parody of heroic sacrifice tropes, Carlton's dramatic nature applied to embarrassing situation

jokes.text: Go on without me. Save yourself.
jokes.setup: Carlton appears to be stuck under Mad Dog
jokes.punchline: Dramatic movie sacrifice dialogue in absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Good parody of dramatic movie moments. Carlton's theatrical delivery in ridiculous situation.

jokes.text: This is just a dream.
jokes.setup: Mad Dog confronting the nearly naked intruders
jokes.punchline: Will's desperate denial of reality
jokes.explanation: Will's panic leading to childish denial, economical expression of desperation

jokes.text: Hey, man. We from L.A. Stripper Gram. Yeah, you know. We got a special birthday wish for you from Tiffany.
jokes.setup: Mad Dog demanding to know who they are
jokes.punchline: Will's desperate lie about being strippers
jokes.explanation: Creative desperate lie that somewhat fits their undressed state, Will's quick thinking under pressure

jokes.text: It's not my birthday.
jokes.setup: Will and Carlton singing happy birthday
jokes.punchline: Mad Dog's flat denial ruining their cover story
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that destroys their elaborate lie with simple fact

jokes.text: Macho, macho man / I've got to be a macho man / Young man, there's a place you can go / I said, young man / You're all out on your own / I said, Y-M-C-A / Y-M-C-A
jokes.setup: Trying to maintain the stripper charade
jokes.punchline: Performing stereotypical male stripper songs badly
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of Will and Carlton doing stripper routine, mixing different songs awkwardly

jokes.text: This black-on-black crime makes me sick.
jokes.setup: Mad Dog threatening violence
jokes.punchline: Will making social commentary about the situation
jokes.explanation: Smart social commentary disguised as desperate stalling tactic, shows Will's intelligence even in panic

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I just saw the state of Florida just pass over my head.
jokes.setup: Carlton getting thrown or hit by Mad Dog
jokes.punchline: Absurd description of how far he was thrown
jokes.explanation: Creative exaggeration of physical impact, using geographical reference for comedic effect

jokes.text: Shouldn't you be climbing up the Empire State Building?
jokes.setup: Mad Dog being very large and intimidating
jokes.punchline: King Kong reference about his size
jokes.explanation: Classic King Kong reference for large person, somewhat predictable but economical

jokes.text: Bet you wish you wouldn't have gone off that Slim Fast now, don't you?
jokes.setup: Carlton having trouble fitting through window
jokes.punchline: Will joking about Carlton's weight loss efforts
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Carlton's fitness concerns, ironic that being thinner creates problems

jokes.text: Shut up and get back in there, you're stretching the building.
jokes.setup: Will making weight jokes about Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton's absurd comeback about stretching the building
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist comeback, Carlton getting in his own architectural joke

jokes.text: Oh. Well, you know, it's almost winter. Eventually, he's gonna have to hibernate.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking what they'll do about Mad Dog
jokes.punchline: Will treating Mad Dog like a wild animal that hibernates
jokes.explanation: Brilliant animal metaphor extension, treating human like bear is unexpected and creative

jokes.text: Oh. Well, you know, it's almost winter. Eventually, he's gonna have to hibernate.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks what they'll do about Mad Dog
jokes.punchline: Will treating Mad Dog like a wild animal with seasonal behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extension of treating Mad Dog as a wild animal. Will's logic is absurd but delivered seriously.

jokes.text: Now, hand me the baby.
jokes.setup: Crisis intervention worker arriving to help
jokes.punchline: Assuming there's a baby in a suicide situation
jokes.explanation: Absurd assumption about the situation, shows incompetence of crisis worker

jokes.text: Sticks and stones will break your bones but a 50-foot fall will kill y'all. I made that up myself.
jokes.setup: Will calling the crisis worker names
jokes.punchline: Crisis worker's twisted version of children's rhyme
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark twist on familiar saying, worker's pride in his creativity adds to absurdity

jokes.text: That civil service exam is really easy, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Crisis worker showing complete incompetence
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic comment about government hiring standards
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary disguised as sarcasm, economical critique of bureaucracy

jokes.text: How am I supposed to know? Who do I look like, Dionne Warwick?
jokes.setup: Discussion about women expecting men to know their feelings
jokes.punchline: Phil referencing psychic reader Dionne Warwick
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture reference to psychic hotline spokesperson, economical and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Sports. Football. Sports.
jokes.setup: Vivian asking what they're discussing
jokes.punchline: All three men giving obviously false answers
jokes.explanation: Good male bonding comedy, their synchronized lying about discussing relationships

jokes.text: I wasn't listening, you know?
jokes.setup: Discussing women asking 'what did I just say?'
jokes.punchline: Will admitting the honest answer no man would give
jokes.explanation: Honest admission that creates relatability, Will's candor about typical male behavior

jokes.text: If you was listening to me, what'd I just say? They always say that. I wasn't listening, you know?
jokes.setup: Discussion of things women say that confuse men
jokes.punchline: Will's honest admission that proves the woman's point
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor with Will admitting the obvious truth. Relatable relationship comedy.

tmdb_overview: Will and Carlton's double date ends unromantically when a big bruiser shows up looking for his squeeze.
